event: Pain paroxysmal and worsened after meals
No history of close contact with infected individuals
Anemia with haemoglobin level of 85 g/L
Hypoalbuminemia with albumin level of 21.5 g/L
Right abdominal tenderness without rebound or guarding
Drainage catheter yielded 150 mL of fulvous fluid
Gas accumulation and dilation in the bowel
Taken to operating room for urgent exploratory laparotomy
Fulvous purulent exudate in extraperitoneal space and abdominal cavity
Necrotic tissue in extraperitoneal space and abdominal cavity
Perforation under right side of liver
Noradrenaline was pumped to maintain blood pressure
Intravenous hydration used to enlarge blood capacity
Blood cultures and drainage fluids sent
Blood samples taken for cultures again
Blood cultures positive for E. lenta
Gram stain revealed short Gram-positive bacillus without spores
Identified as E. lenta by MALDI-TOF MS
Cultures of drainage fluid grew Escherichia coli
Repeat CT revealed decreased hepatic abscess size and decreased ascites
Administered ertapenem and teicoplanin for another 14 days
After further 7-day course of intravenous ertapenem and teicoplanin, symptoms achieved further alleviation
Discharged from hospital with prescription for oral antibiotics (clindamycin)
Remained well as outpatient without recurrence of fever
Complete resolution of abscess was found at follow-up
